title: When millions of AI agents meet
summary: Nenad Tomašev discusses the theoretical framework of an agentic economy where millions of AI agents transact and delegate with each other. The conversation covers the operational shift from single systems to a society of specialists, automation bias risks, and distributed intelligence security challenges including agent traps and dynamic cloaking.
writeup: Nenad Tomašev lays out a theoretical framework for an agentic economy in which millions of AI agents transact and delegate tasks among themselves. The discussion traces the operational shift from single systems to a society of specialists coordinating through multi-step planning and delegation across distributed processing. It examines the failure modes of such a system, including automation bias and distributed intelligence security challenges such as agent traps and dynamic cloaking. The takeaway is that scaling from individual agents to interacting populations introduces coordination and security problems distinct from those of standalone models.
